items.title: Zelensky returns Polish award as WWII row strains alliance
items.standfirst: Ukraine and Poland's close wartime partnership has been shaken by a growing dispute over World War II history, with President Volodymyr Zelensky and senior Ukrainian officials returning Polish state honours after Warsaw stripped him of its highest award.
items.content: Ukraine and Poland's close wartime partnership has been shaken by a growing dispute over World War II history, with President Volodymyr Zelensky and senior Ukrainian officials returning Polish state honours after Warsaw stripped him of its highest award.

items.title: Poland strips Zelensky of top state honour over WWII dispute
items.standfirst: Poland has withdrawn its highest state honour from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, after Kyiv named an army unit after a nationalist group that massacred Polish civilians during World War II. The decision by President Karol Nawrocki threatens to unleash a serious diplomatic crisis between the two neighbours – just days before a major conference on Ukraine's reconstruction is due to be held in the Polish city of Gdańsk. Story by Charlotte Lam and Antoine Fenaux.
items.content: Poland has withdrawn its highest state honour from Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky, after Kyiv named an army unit after a nationalist group that massacred Polish civilians during World War II. The decision by President Karol Nawrocki threatens to unleash a serious diplomatic crisis between the two neighbours – just days before a major conference on Ukraine's reconstruction is due to be held in the Polish city of Gdańsk. Story by Charlotte Lam and Antoine Fenaux.

Poland's president revoked the honor due to Ukraine's stance

items.title: Trump says Italy's Meloni asked 'over and over' for a photo with him as diplomatic spat escalates
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump turned up the heat on a diplomatic row with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Saturday, accusing her of repeatedly seeking a photograph with him at the G7 summit and blaming the clash between the two heads of state on tensions over Iran and NATO. Meloni later fired back, slamming the 'constant, unprovoked attacks' from Trump as 'senseless'.
items.content: US President Donald Trump turned up the heat on a diplomatic row with Italian Prime Minister Giorgia Meloni on Saturday, accusing her of repeatedly seeking a photograph with him at the G7 summit and blaming the clash between the two heads of state on tensions over Iran and NATO. Meloni later fired back, slamming the 'constant, unprovoked attacks' from Trump as 'senseless'.

items.title: Ueda shines as Japan knocks out Tunisia in World Cup's 1,000th game
items.standfirst: The World Cup marked its 1000th game with Japan thrashing Tunisia 4-0 on Saturday to close in on a place in the last 32. Ayase Ueda scored twice while Daichi Kamada and Junya Ito were also on target as the Asian giants joined the Netherlands on four points at the top of Group F.
items.content: The World Cup marked its 1000th game with Japan thrashing Tunisia 4-0 on Saturday to close in on a place in the last 32. Ayase Ueda scored twice while Daichi Kamada and Junya Ito were also on target as the Asian giants joined the Netherlands on four points at the top of Group F.

clusters.title: The happiest World Cup game
clusters.neutral_headline: World Cup Teams Ranked By Happiness

items.title: The happiest World Cup game
items.content: POLITICO has been crunching the numbers to see how all 48 of this year’s World Cup participants rank in several other off-field categories, which we’ll share more of over the weekend. In today’s item, we're looking at countries sorted by their FIFA rank against their citizens' assessment of how close they are to living their best possible life. Turns out, the happiest game of the World Cup will be Sweden vs. the Netherlands today (that’s also the only group-stage game taking place between two EU members) — while the upcoming game between Haiti and Morocco on June 25 may well be interrupted by floods of tears and bouts of introspection.

items.title: World cup wrap day 9

items.content: World cup wrap day 9

clusters.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
clusters.neutral_headline: African and Caribbean nations seek formal apology

items.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
items.standfirst: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.
items.content: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

items.title: Global framework for reparatory justice adopted at landmark Ghana conference
items.standfirst: Call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from transatlantic trade slave included in 19-point roadmapMore than money: the logic of slavery reparationsA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade.Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...
items.content: Call for formal apologies from countries that benefited from transatlantic trade slave included in 19-point roadmapMore than money: the logic of slavery reparationsA global framework for reparatory justice has been adopted at a conference in Ghana, as African and Caribbean leaders demanded formal apologies from countries that benefited from the transatlantic slave trade.Heads of state and government and other officials formally approved the strategy on Friday at a gathering in a hotel in the capital, Accra, which was the first major meeting since the adoption of the landmark United Nations (UN) resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans as the gravest crime against humanity. Continue reading...
